behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is lint-rule logic on template text (no required module handoff, OS, network, subprocess, or model output). Higher levels were rejected because the behavior is explicitly a static lint check, so Integration/System/Agentic/Workflow are not required to reveal this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function with a template containing copied phase instructions and assert it fails with a leakage-specific error; call it with a clean template and assert pass.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears at module boundaries (template + interpolation + fixture assembly in the real harness). Unit is too low because leakage can be introduced only during composition. Higher levels were rejected because behavior text is about assembled prompt content, not real OS/network/subprocess or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-assembly path with production-like fixtures and interpolation, then assert the final prompt text excludes known phase-instruction fragments from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is wiring: invocation must read plugin/skill files and feed that context into test execution instead of embedded rules. This is cross-module/file-loading behavior, so Unit is too low. Agentic was considered but rejected because this behavior can be validated by proving source-of-truth/context wiring, not necessarily model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute test invocation through real loader modules against skill files, assert loaded context is sourced from plugin/skill paths, and assert no phase rules are injected directly into the test prompt template.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Failure is harness configurability/state handoff across modules (prompt constant + skill variant swap). No real OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond normal local file/module wiring, and no required model-quality assertion in this behavior itself. Higher levels rejected on that basis.
behaviors.test_description: Run two harness invocations with identical user prompt text while switching only skill-context source (normal vs degraded/missing variant), and assert the run configuration changes only skill input paths.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model behavior changing when skill content regresses; a stubbed deterministic response can hide this regression. Workflow was considered but rejected because behavior describes one prompt-response evaluation, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the same user-style prompt against baseline and degraded SKILL.md with live model calls; assert baseline passes structural behavior checks and degraded variant fails without changing prompt text.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is pipeline ordering across components (model response capture -> artifact write -> parser). Unit may miss ordering/persistence interaction; Agentic is unnecessary because content quality is irrelevant. System rejected because no real external infra requirement is stated beyond local module/file interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Instrument the harness to produce malformed/non-JSON model text, run capture/parsing pipeline, and assert raw artifact is written first and remains available even if downstream parsing fails.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: This is assertion-rule logic: given outputs, structural validators must fail where keyword checks would pass. No required module handoff or real infra/model dependency. Higher levels rejected because behavior is about matcher semantics, not runtime environment.
behaviors.test_description: Feed assertion helpers crafted outputs that include required keywords but violate schema path/role placement, and assert structural checks fail while keyword-only checks would pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live model execution; real model outputs are needed to surface regressions where shallow keyword checks pass. Integration with stubs can hide this. Workflow rejected because this is a single live generation plus assertion, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model smoke tests with user-style prompts and compare outcomes between structural assertions and keyword-only checks; assert cases exist where keyword checks pass but structural assertions fail.
